concepts.name: System Explainability
concepts.description: The mechanism by which the control plane exposes internal model state to human operators for debugging and verification.

concepts.name: Post-hoc Explanations
concepts.description: Computationally intensive interpretability methods, like SHAP or LIME, run after inference to clarify individual predictions.

concepts.name: Saliency Maps
concepts.description: Lightweight explanation methods using single backward passes on input gradients, suitable for latency-sensitive edge environments.

concepts.name: Demographic Parity
concepts.description: A fairness metric requiring the probability of a positive prediction to be entirely independent of group membership.

concepts.name: Equalized Odds
concepts.description: A fairness metric ensuring both true positive and false positive rates are equal across protected groups.

concepts.name: Equality of Opportunity
concepts.description: A fairness constraint focusing solely on ensuring the true positive rate is equal across different demographic groups.

concepts.name: Fairness Impossibility Law
concepts.description: The mathematical proof that Demographic Parity, Equalized Odds, and Calibration cannot be simultaneously satisfied when group base rates differ.

concepts.name: Intersectional Fairness
concepts.description: The evaluation of model performance across overlapping identity attributes to prevent compounded biases.

concepts.name: Disparate Impact Ratio
concepts.description: A quantitative metric representing the ratio of favorable outcome rates between unprivileged and privileged groups.

concepts.name: Statistical Parity Difference
concepts.description: The absolute difference in positive outcome selection rates between privileged and unprivileged groups.

concepts.name: Model Memorization
concepts.description: The tendency of models to encode specific details from training data, creating privacy vulnerabilities queryable at inference.

concepts.name: Membership Inference Attacks
concepts.description: Techniques used to determine if a specific individual's data was part of a model's training dataset by analyzing outputs.

concepts.name: Adversarial Inputs
concepts.description: Carefully constructed input perturbations designed to cause a machine learning model to output incorrect or harmful predictions.

concepts.name: Distribution Shift
concepts.description: The degradation of model reliability caused by divergence between training and deployment data distributions.

concepts.name: Model Cards
concepts.description: Standardized technical documentation providing benchmarked evaluation across specific cultural and demographic groups for trained models.

concepts.name: Datasheets for Datasets
concepts.description: Standardized documentation detailing the motivation, composition, collection process, and recommended uses of a dataset.

concepts.name: Fairness-aware Training
concepts.description: In-processing methods that embed group fairness constraints directly into the optimization objective.

concepts.name: Multicalibration
concepts.description: An advanced strategy ensuring model predictions remain calibrated across a wide range of intersecting subgroups.

concepts.name: Data Anonymization Layer
concepts.description: An inference-path component applying privacy-preserving transformations like k-anonymity or differential privacy noise.

concepts.name: Real-time Fairness Monitoring
concepts.description: Telemetry infrastructure computing rolling fairness metrics during live inference to detect demographic disparities.

concepts.name: Machine Unlearning
concepts.description: The algorithmic process of removing the statistical influence of specific training data records from model weights.
